United Kingdom

Fully Funded UK Chevening Scholarship for Migrant Students

Published 28 Jun 20266 min read✍ MigrantScholar Team✓ Verified 28 Jun 2026
Advertisement

Introduction to Fully Funded UK Chevening Scholarship

The UK Chevening Scholarship is a fully funded opportunity for migrant students, refugees, and asylum seekers to pursue a Master's degree in the United Kingdom. This prestigious scholarship covers full tuition fees, a monthly living allowance, and other expenses for the duration of the program, typically one year. By applying for the Chevening Scholarship, eligible students can achieve their academic goals without financial burden.

Who Qualifies

To be eligible for the UK Chevening Scholarship, migrant students must hold a valid visa under one of the following categories:

* Tier 4 (General) student visa

* Tier 2 (General) work visa

* Family visa

* Refugee or asylum seeker status

* Humanitarian protection status

* Discretionary leave to remain

* Leave outside the rules

Available Scholarships

The following five scholarships are available for migrant students to apply:

Chevening Scholarship

The Chevening Scholarship covers £18,000 to £20,000 per year, depending on the course and institution. Eligible students must have a Bachelor's degree, two years of work experience, and meet the English language proficiency requirements. The deadline for application is typically in November of each year. For more information, visit the official Chevening website.

Commonwealth Shared Scholarship

The Commonwealth Shared Scholarship provides £10,000 to £15,000 per year for students from commonwealth countries. To be eligible, students must have a Bachelor's degree, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and not have studied in the UK before. The deadline for application is typically in December of each year. For more information, visit the official Commonwealth website.

University of Manchester Scholarship

The University of Manchester Scholarship offers £12,000 to £18,000 per year for international students, including migrants. Eligible students must have a Bachelor's degree, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and be accepted into a Master's program at the University of Manchester. The deadline for application is typically in June of each year. For more information, visit the official University of Manchester website.

University of Edinburgh Scholarship

The University of Edinburgh Scholarship provides £10,000 to £15,000 per year for international students, including migrants. To be eligible, students must have a Bachelor's degree, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and be accepted into a Master's program at the University of Edinburgh. The deadline for application is typically in April of each year. For more information, visit the official University of Edinburgh website.

Imperial College London Scholarship

The Imperial College London Scholarship offers £12,000 to £20,000 per year for international students, including migrants. Eligible students must have a Bachelor's degree, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and be accepted into a Master's program at Imperial College London. The deadline for application is typically in March of each year. For more information, visit the official Imperial College London website.

Application Steps

To apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship, follow these steps:

  • Check your eligibility and visa category to ensure you qualify for the scholarship.

  • Choose your preferred Master's program and institution in the UK.

  • Review the scholarship requirements and deadlines.

  • Prepare and submit your application, including all required documents and information.

  • Take the English language proficiency test, if required.

  • Submit your application before the deadline.

  • Wait for the application to be reviewed and processed.

  • Receive the outcome of your application and begin preparing for your studies in the UK.

Documents Checklist

When applying for the UK Chevening Scholarship, ensure you have the following documents:

* Valid passport

* Visa category documentation

* Bachelor's degree certificate

* Transcripts

* English language proficiency test results

* Two years of work experience documentation

* Personal statement

* References

* Proof of financial support

Frequently Asked Questions

  • What is the UK Chevening Scholarship, and who is eligible to apply?

The UK Chevening Scholarship is a fully funded opportunity for migrant students, refugees, and asylum seekers to pursue a Master's degree in the UK. To be eligible, students must meet the specific requirements, including holding a valid visa under one of the eligible categories.

  • How do I apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship, and what documents do I need to provide?

To apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship, students must submit their application, including all required documents and information, before the deadline. The required documents include a valid passport, visa category documentation, Bachelor's degree certificate, and English language proficiency test results.

  • Can I apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship if I have already studied in the UK?

It depends on the specific scholarship and institution. Some scholarships, such as the Commonwealth Shared Scholarship, require students not to have studied in the UK before. However, other scholarships, such as the Chevening Scholarship, do not have this requirement.

  • How long does it take to process the UK Chevening Scholarship application, and when can I expect to receive the outcome?

The application process for the UK Chevening Scholarship typically takes several months. Students can expect to receive the outcome of their application within 6-8 months after submitting their application.

  • Can I apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship if I have a dependent, such as a spouse or child?

Yes, students with dependents can apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship. However, they must provide additional documentation and information, such as proof of financial support for their dependents.

  • How much does the UK Chevening Scholarship cover, and what expenses are included?

The UK Chevening Scholarship covers full tuition fees, a monthly living allowance, and other expenses for the duration of the program, typically one year.

  • Can I apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship if I have a conditional offer from a UK institution?

Yes, students with a conditional offer from a UK institution can apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship. However, they must meet the conditions of their offer before they can begin their studies.

  • What happens if my UK Chevening Scholarship application is unsuccessful, and can I reapply?

If a student's application is unsuccessful, they can reapply in the following year. However, they must review and improve their application, ensuring they meet the eligibility requirements and submit a strong application.

For more information on the UK Chevening Scholarship, visit the official UK government website. Additionally, students can visit the University of Oxford website to explore available Master's programs and scholarships.

To learn more about scholarship opportunities for migrant students, visit our blog at https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog.
